threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of just 'the train'—everyone knows it’s all about the 30-minute journeys to Shoreditch.
Caught a guy on the 133 rehearsing Shakespeare soliloquies. He was so into it, even the driver started clapping at the next stop. Perfectly Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a couple arguing in front of a kebab shop, then the shop owner stepped out, handed them a plate of fries, and they hugged it out.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find 24-hour Späti with fresh-baked bread at 3 AM. A sleepy locals' secret amidst the late-night crowds.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they know to avoid the 14-Mission during BART delays—unlike newcomers, they won't be caught waiting at a stop for ages.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 bus packed tighter than a sardine can—an opera singer turned a mundane commute into a one-act show. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for partying, but locals cherish the calm of the corners and the scent of homemade paella wafting from hidden kitchens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to try the local bakeries; they’re still clutching those foil-wrapped croissants from France.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver shouts 'next stop' and the whole bus mutters back, like some strange ritual.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded everyone with his guitar. Surprisingly decent, but the best bit was the old lady who kept telling him to play 'Wonderwall'.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a massive street mural of a cat wearing sunglasses and realized the neighborhood vibe is unapologetically quirky.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous raves in abandoned buildings where strangers become friends over shared beats and brews.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their casual dismissal of the newest burrito joint’s hype; real ones still rave about the hole-in-the-wall on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just witnessed a guy serenading a entire bus with a ukulele at 5th and Market. The driver smiled, but the passengers were mostly confused.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often don’t realize that the best vermut in Gràcia is found in unmarked places, not the trendy bars on the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking selfies with the giant mosaic lizard; locals know it’s just a photo op, not a destination.
